title: landolphia glandulosa
intro: Landolphia glandulosa, also known as the African yam or African cherry, is a species of evergreen tree native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Landolphia glandulosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and ointments, and it is also used in traditional African medicine to treat a range of ailments.
